title: Informed consent — laparoscopic cholecystectomy
candidateInstructions: You are an FY2 doctor in the surgical pre-assessment clinic. Mrs Jennifer Liu, a 43-year-old teacher, is scheduled for elective laparoscopic cholecystectomy next week for symptomatic gallstones. She has been seen by the consultant but has not yet been consented. Please consent her for this procedure.
examinerNotes: Surgical consent station. Candidate must explain: procedure (4 small incisions, CO2, camera, removal of gallbladder), benefits (resolution of symptoms, prevent complications), risks (bleeding, infection, bile duct injury 0.3%, bile leak, conversion to open, anaesthetic). Must give opportunity to ask questions. Must not use excessive jargon.

patient.name: Jennifer Liu
patient.presentingComplaint: Here to sign consent for gallbladder surgery
patient.reasonForAttendance: I'm having my gallbladder out next week and need to sign the consent form

hpi.character: Episodic right upper quadrant colicky pain, worse after fatty food
associatedSymptoms: One episode of jaundice last year
hpi.severity: Severe during attacks 8/10, attacks lasting 4-6 hours

examination.general: Well woman, not in pain today

patient.emotionalState: Anxious about the operation. Concerned about risks. Has heard 'keyhole surgery' is routine but worried about complications. Will ask several questions.
patient.communicationStyle: Intelligent, asks direct questions. Will ask: 'What if it goes wrong? What are the chances of something serious?', 'How long will I be off work?', 'Is there any other option?'
hiddenConcerns: What if they damage something while they're in there?
How long will I need off work? I can't leave my class for too long
My sister had complications after her gallbladder surgery
doNotVolunteer: Codeine allergy — only if asked about allergies
Concerns about anaesthetic — only if doctor asks about concerns or worries
Sister's complications — only if asked about family surgical history

primaryDiagnosis: Symptomatic Cholelithiasis (gallstones) — for elective laparoscopic cholecystectomy

dataGathering.criterion: Checks patient understanding of planned procedure
dataGathering.detailedExpectation: Asks what patient understands about the operation

dataGathering.criterion: Confirms allergies and relevant medical history
dataGathering.detailedExpectation: Confirms codeine allergy, asthma — relevant to anaesthesia

clinicalManagement.criterion: Explains procedure clearly in lay terms
clinicalManagement.detailedExpectation: 4 small cuts, camera, CO2 gas, clips/staples on bile duct and artery, removes gallbladder through small incision
Relief of pain, prevention of serious complications (cholangitis, pancreatitis)
Bleeding, infection, hernia, general anaesthetic risks

clinicalManagement.criterion: Explains serious specific risks including bile duct injury
clinicalManagement.detailedExpectation: Bile duct injury (0.3%), conversion to open operation, bile leak

clinicalManagement.criterion: Discusses recovery and return to work
clinicalManagement.detailedExpectation: Typically 1-2 weeks off work, full recovery 2-3 weeks

interpersonalSkills.detailedExpectation: Introduces self and purpose of appointment

interpersonalSkills.criterion: Uses lay language throughout — minimal jargon

interpersonalSkills.detailedExpectation: Creates opportunity for patient to ask questions, answers appropriately
Confirms patient can withdraw at any time, decision is voluntary

references.source: GMC: Consent — patients and doctors making decisions together (2008)
Montgomery v Lanarkshire Health Board [2015] — patient-specific risk disclosure

learningObjectives: Obtain valid informed consent for a surgical procedure
Disclose material risks including bile duct injury
